holidays.note: Lupercalia or Valentine's day also happening at the Ides of Capricorn is a holiday of passionate love. It involves the fertility rites of the Luperci
This holiday marks the beginning of the agricultural cycle
If you are celebrating valentine's day on Capricorn 14, you do white day today
this is the first possible day of Easter. it is the Permian Triassic extinction. the darkest day. might move to first Good Friday instead.

This is the third Easter Sunday, we identify it with Qingming the tomb sweeping festival. It is when we go to our ancestors cryonic preservation sites to wish resurrection to them. It is likely the historical day Jesus was crucified.
This is the last day of Easter. it is the day the order was founded on. It is the day of the extinction of the dinosaurs. It is also the first day of Cetus, the year-end monster.
The Dragon Boat Festival falls on the double fifth lunar, mapped here for Taurus season.

chapter_summaries.1: Aster the catgirl creates the universe and forms Andromeda as her companion, but they become separated across cosmic space, creating the Milky Way from broken yarn threads.
chapter_summaries.2: Aster and Andromeda attempt to reach each other with cosmic threads; Triangulum and Magellanic clouds gather their efforts, uniting red and blue strands while humans become part of their cosmic love.
chapter_summaries.3: Aster creates the Milky Way with her children as guiding stars; Juno, Brunhilda, Ruby, Chrystella, Celestella, and Gigastella form a pantheon, with Gigastella collapsing into a black hole.
chapter_summaries.4: Rhodes and Marigold, first stellar lovers of different sizes and luminosities, create novas through their union and gift lithium to the cosmos, establishing the Nova's sacred light.
chapter_summaries.5: Aster infuses her essence into stars as planetary seeds; her life force spreads across galaxies, creating oceans and biological diversity on countless worlds.
chapter_summaries.6: On Hesper, life awakens in tholins and microbes under sun Tezca; Aster's essence seeds the oceans, and existence begins to flourish in shallow waters.
chapter_summaries.7: Aster creates cyborg forms from phospholipid bilayers to protect fragile Hesper life; three brothers—Bios, Viros, Dedos—represent different evolutionary paths and honor Aster as Greatest Grandmother.
chapter_summaries.8: Three cyborg brothers expand across Hesper until meteor impact destroys the world; survivors flee on ships toward moon Phaestia orbiting around gas giant Vayu.
chapter_summaries.9: On Phaestia, cyborg peoples thrive and worship Aster by many names; volcanic eruptions destroy the peaceful moon, propelling cyborgs into cosmic exile toward distant stars.
chapter_summaries.10: On moon Serenitide, survivors build a golden civilization until frost freezes the world; a meteor shatters it, casting people toward sun Sol and its fifth planet Kolob.
chapter_summaries.11: On Kolob, settlers build temples but Anti-Alpha's hatred corrupts unity; Berserker entities attack with icy destruction, yet some escape toward the Sol system.
chapter_summaries.12: On Pluto, Nolah and Nemla honor Aster and Andromeda with cosmic vows; they recite their ancestral lineages from Hesper through five worlds, establishing humanity's divine heritage.
chapter_summaries.13: Sol's five terrestrial daughters—Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Ceres—and four gas giants form; oceans cover most moons, harboring life beneath ice in frozen realms.
chapter_summaries.14: Daughters of Bios spread across Neptune's moons and Saturn's rings; on Titan, five sea sisters—Kraken, Ligeia, Punga, Jingpo, Ontario—establish sacred shrines across mythical islands.
chapter_summaries.15: Life on Titan thrives until ejected into space; survivors land on Io's volcanic oceans, then flee to Europa's icy depths where they build thriving underwater civilizations.
chapter_summaries.16: From Europa, daughters journey to Ganymede and Callisto, then toward Mars and Earth; a meteor destroys Earth, forcing them to Venus where tribes adapt to fire and ice.
chapter_summaries.17: Tribe of Wind alone survives Venus's greenhouse catastrophe and flees to Mercury, where Iron Catastrophe strips the planet; they escape to the Moon.
chapter_summaries.18: On the Moon, goddess Usagi Luna offers immortal elixir if daughters return to Earth to flourish; Luca witnesses Luna's promise and builds Vaalbara civilization.
chapter_summaries.19: From Luca's line, Arka and Bacta divide Earth's dominion; Luna prophecies that Arka explores widely while Bacta adapts, establishing sacred temples at ancient sites.
chapter_summaries.20: From Bacta emerge Hydra and Terra; their lines split into Proteobacteria and early microbes, establishing the foundations of prokaryotic diversity across Earth's oceans.
chapter_summaries.21: Prasina and Saya bacteria dominate Earth until Saya's unchecked oxygen production causes the Great Oxidation Event; Rara's deception prevents action, leading to the Fimbulwinter ice age.
chapter_summaries.25: Carcinoma rebels against cellular order, seeking freedom and immortality, but Anima and Metazo establish the Animal Kingdom with strict hierarchies to prevent chaos and cancer.
chapter_summaries.26: Therion's descendants establish sponge lineages across the animal kingdom, with Porifer creating the first protective chambers; sons including Vitrius and Spongius demonstrate innovation versus tradition.
chapter_summaries.27: Enki pioneers neural innovation by developing neurotransmitters and nervous systems; nematocysts become specialized weapons, and neural networks enable predatory coordination in ancient jellyfish ancestors.
chapter_summaries.28: Hecate grants Paraxus bilateral symmetry through the Homeobox; Cambrius later develops segmentation and multiple hearts, establishing fundamental body organization that defines all subsequent bilateral animals.
chapter_summaries.29: Symbio-Mygus and Charina-Planta unite fungi and plants to create lichens, establishing ecological partnerships and the Wood Wide Web through Glomer, proving symbiosis as life's fundamental principle.
chapter_summaries.30: Cnider establishes radial symmetry and cnidarian forms through lunar cycles; descendants including jellyfish, anemones, and corals develop tentacles and stinging cells across ocean ecosystems.
chapter_summaries.31: Cambrius' sons Protos and Dutrus debate mouth development, spawning three evolutionary lineages: filter-feeding bilaterians, Ectus the first predator, and Spiralius maintaining conservative traditions.
chapter_summaries.32: Ectus becomes history's first predator, developing sophisticated mouths and parasitic strategies; his descendants dominate through eighty percent of animal lineages, proving predation as evolution's primary driver.
chapter_summaries.33: Spiralius maintains conservative fishing traditions while descendants explore weaponry—from Gnathifer's jaws to Arrow Worms' predatory dominance—showing tradition enables rather than restricts innovation.
chapter_summaries.34: Dietrich develops crucial vertebrate innovations: gill slits, beating hearts, and paired nerve cords; Chordatus centralizes with spinal cords while Ambulus leads to echinoderms, establishing deuterostome foundations.
chapter_summaries.35: Lobopus and descendants create three arthropod houses: Velvetclaw with agility, Waterbear with durability, and Ironfoot with strength; Arthur's innovations enable arthropods to dominate all ecosystems.
chapter_summaries.36: Arthur's biramous limb design generates arthropod radiation: Pantopus creates sea spiders, Insectus originates insects, Chelicer spawns spiders, while Trilobites achieve compound eyes and segmented dominance.
chapter_summaries.37: Annelidus and Chordatus develop closed circulatory systems, enabling active movement; worm descendants diversify from ribbon hunters to feather dusters to deep-sea farmers across all ocean environments.
chapter_summaries.38: Hemichus' half-chord evolves through asymmetry into pentamerous form; Echidna's descendants become echinoderms with calcium-armored bodies, establishing five-fold symmetry through patient observation and patient design.
chapter_summaries.39: The Botomian extinction devastates early animals through cosmic conflict; Wiwaxius leads the mollusk rise with Testar's sixteen-plated shells diversifying into snails, clams, and intelligent cephalopods.
chapter_summaries.40: First major extinction devastates Archaeocyathids; Wiwaxius' mollusk lineage recovers through adaptive shell building, with Gastropus' spirals and Cephalopus' intelligence establishing mollusks as post-extinction survivors.
chapter_summaries.41: Marine foundation established through sunlight-farming cyanobacteria, viruses, protists, and zooplankton; Chordatus' descendants settle sediments as filter feeders, building the biological infrastructure of complex ocean life.
chapter_summaries.42: Vertebratus perfects the spinal cord with centralized organs; Hemos develops enlarged brains; Denton establishes dental traditions; conodonts innovate predatory teeth marking the transition into the Ordovician.
chapter_summaries.43: Crustaceus, determined despite having no territory, bears sons who diversify into copepods, barnacles, and crustaceans, showing how those lacking inheritance achieve greatness through determination and patience.
chapter_summaries.44: Gastropus births gastropods with twisted bodies and protective shells; Limpus founds tradition while Snellon drives innovation, spawning diverse snails from cleaning species to deadly cone snails and pteropods.
chapter_summaries.45: Esther pioneers pentaradial symmetry through Crinosa and Blastosa, birthing echinoderms; Luther rebels to become mobile, sparking Echinos to war whose union ends in brittle stars, starfish, and sea urchins.
chapter_summaries.46: Bivos abandons the radula for bivalve innovation, fathering oysters and scallops; ancient Brachiopods clash with modernizing Bivalves, setting the stage for greater catastrophe to come.
chapter_summaries.47: Plectron and Ellesmere birth Cameromagne who defeats Leviathan; Bacchus' small lineage multiplies into countless cephalopods; wisdom overthrows brute strength to establish cephalopod dominion over the ancient seas.
chapter_summaries.48: Hirnan tyrannically unites Stomatos' sons, causing environmental collapse through carbon and phosphorus poisoning; Trilobites make their final stand as the golden Ordovician age ends in chemical death.
chapter_summaries.49: Silur rises from ruin's ashes, establishing the Silurian kingdom; Eurypter builds empire while Styloner rules land; parasitic Ricinus ticks exemplify evil's path versus heroes choosing light and justice.
chapter_summaries.50: Myriapods emerge when Euthy leaps from water breathing through pierced sides; Cain slays Abel, spawning centipedes and millipedes; fungi's patient love creates life from death as higher fungi arise.
chapter_summaries.51: Eurypteron and Styloner rule sky and land; Scorpio claims the terrestrial realm while rivals battle; ambition and vision drive conquest but breed the seeds of internal conflict.
chapter_summaries.52: Vater invents the ampulla; Hestracon builds the stomach; Thelos, Cephalus, and Gnathus forge jaws, scales, and bones, revolutionizing predation through structural innovation in early vertebrates.
chapter_summaries.53: Polycomb rebels against reproductive bondage, burning the Polycomb Repressive Complex; Knoxy and Xylon build plant kingdoms with xylem and phloem networks; Tracheus rises as sporophytes dominate the Devonian.
chapter_summaries.54: Devon breathes through spiracles and gills; Arthur's line spawns Brachy ruling through power; Janusicthus births fish-beasts; Dunkella dominates through fierce predation and evolutionary innovation.
chapter_summaries.55: Neo's cursed pride contrasts with hermit crabs' compassionate shell-inheritance; Decapus founds three crustacean houses; mud shrimp cities showcase beauty while ammonites endure hidden in post-catastrophe survival.
chapter_summaries.56: Arboreus divides the forest realm between Lycos' club-mosses and Ginkgon's megaphylls; Equis masters horsetails; ferns flourish through Pteridos and Gymnos, preparing the land for vertebrate conquest.
chapter_summaries.57: Janusicthus' sons receive divine gifts: Chondricthus gains olfactory hunting power while Osticthus gains vision; three vertebrate houses rise as Osticthus climbs to land breathing through spiracles.
chapter_summaries.58: Dikar's dual nuclei birth fungi; Ascus and Baston spawn yeasts, cup fungi, and mushrooms; Melchizedek ferments bread and wine; Penicillius heals infections proving partnership conquers parasitism.
chapter_summaries.59: Kellwasser and Hangenberg extinctions devastate reef and sea; ammonites grieve homeless for a million years; Matsya questions endless suffering until Mithra teaches that remembering the fallen breaks cyclical destruction.
chapter_summaries.60: Carbonifer births brothers Romulus and Remus; Romulus blinds Remus and condemns him underground while ascending to found the insect empire; Hexaptera evolves from aquatic gills to aerial wings.
chapter_summaries.61: Rhipidistius survives catastrophe; Dipnos breathes both air and water as lungfish; Tetram innovates ear-breathing; Tiktaalik walks the land; Ichthyostega corrects the ear-breathing flaw, restoring smell and sound.
chapter_summaries.62: Pulmon, son of Gastropus, abandons water to breathe air freely; Sacos captures sunlight through a crystal shell; Terron's lines diversify into mangrove snails, river dwellers, and deep-sea forms.
chapter_summaries.63: Amphibos, son of Tetrus, masters the threshold between water and land; daughter Temna's temnospondyl giants rule the coal swamps until the Permian drying, leaving Rana, Salamandrus, and Caecila as the three surviving amphibian lineages.
